macro_context.headline: UAE intercepts Iranian missiles in first activation of defense system since ceasefire — oil surges, safe havens bid, stocks retreat on Hormuz disruption risk.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD slipped 30 pips to 1.1695 as oil-driven inflation fears pushed rate-cut expectations further out — Eurozone CPI hit 3% on the energy spike.
EURUSD.why: ECB is trapped: spiking energy costs feed headline inflation but crush growth. Markets now price zero cuts before September, making the euro a carry victim.
EURUSD.watch: EZ services PMI final at 11:00 TRT — any downward revision below 50 reopens recession talk and could crack 1.1650 support.

headlines.title: Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
Eurozone inflation hits 3% as oil prices spike and economic growth slows
ECB, Bank of England Set to Hold Rates Unchanged Today

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Cable shed 40 pips to 1.3540 tracking broad risk-off; gilt yields rose on BoE rate-hold expectations as UK fuel costs jumped.
GBPUSD.why: BoE faces the same energy-inflation bind as the ECB, but UK services inflation was already stickier — markets see no cut until Q4.
GBPUSD.watch: UK services PMI at 11:30 TRT. Below 52 signals demand cracking under energy costs; above keeps BoE hawkish and cable range-bound.

headlines.title: The British Pound Is a Buy Now Over the U.S. Dollar as Iran War Continues

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY pushed to 157.20 as US yields rose on oil-driven inflation fears, widening the rate gap despite yen's safe-haven appeal.
USDJPY.why: The BoJ is trapped at ultra-low rates while Fed cuts recede. Verbal intervention risk rises every day above 155 but no action yet.
USDJPY.watch: Japan holiday today thins liquidity. Any headline escalation in the Strait could trigger an intervention-like flush below 155.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD dropped 56 pips to 0.7157 — the sharpest daily fall in two weeks — as iron ore slid on China demand fears and risk-off weighed.
AUDUSD.why: Australia is a net energy importer despite coal exports; higher oil costs squeeze the current account. China stimulus hopes are the only bid left.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA rate decision tomorrow at 06:30 TRT. Consensus hold, but any hawkish tilt on inflation could bounce AUD back above 0.72.

headlines.title: Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?

Dollar subdued as markets eye ceasefire talks; yen pressured by BOJ delay

XAUUSD.name: Gold / US Dollar
XAUUSD.past: Gold surged $46 (+1%) to $4,565 as UAE-Iran missile exchange triggered safe-haven buying and central bank demand remained unrelenting.
XAUUSD.why: Geopolitical premium is back: any Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens global energy supply, and gold is the only hedge that works against both inflation and tail risk.
XAUUSD.watch: If Iran retaliates further overnight, $4,600 resistance breaks. De-escalation headlines would trigger profit-taking toward $4,500 support.

XAGUSD.name: Silver / US Dollar
XAGUSD.past: Silver rallied 1.4% to $74.12, outpacing gold as industrial users rushed to secure supply chains ahead of potential shipping disruptions.
XAGUSD.why: Silver's dual role — safe haven plus industrial metal — gives it extra torque when geopolitics threaten supply routes. Solar panel demand remains the structural floor.
XAGUSD.watch: Gold/silver ratio near 61.5; a break below 60 would signal silver entering a catch-up phase targeting $78.

USOIL.name: WTI Crude Oil
USOIL.past: WTI crude swung violently — initially surging above $106 on UAE-Iran missile news before settling at $103.46 as Trump pledged Hormuz safe passage.
USOIL.why: The Strait of Hormuz carries 20% of global oil. Any closure, even temporary, reprices crude by $20+. The risk premium won't fade until a verified ceasefire.
USOIL.watch: Pentagon deployment updates through the day. Any confirmed tanker rerouting = immediate spike above $110. Diplomatic breakthrough = flush to $95.

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 7,201 as energy-driven inflation fears offset another strong week of AI earnings. The index still sits just 0.4% off its record.
US500.why: Earnings remain the bull case — AI capex spend is real and accelerating — but an oil shock above $110 reprices the Fed path and breaks the narrative.
US500.watch: ISM services PMI at 17:00 TRT. A print above 55 with rising prices-paid component would confirm the stagflation scare; below 53 calms nerves.

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 dipped just 0.2% to 27,652 — megacap tech's defensive quality shone as semis and software held gains from Friday's earnings beat.
NAS100.why: AI capex commitments from MSFT, META, GOOGL are locking in 2027 revenue for the semicap chain. Tech is the only sector with true earnings visibility right now.
NAS100.watch: Broadcom earnings after close Wednesday. Any AI revenue guidance raise above $15B extends the NAS100 breakout; a miss risks a crowded-trade unwind.

US30.name: Dow Jones 30
US30.past: Dow shed 557 points (-1.1%) to 48,942 — the worst day in three weeks — dragged by energy-exposed industrials and transport stocks.
US30.why: The Dow's old-economy tilt makes it the most oil-sensitive US index. Airlines, logistics, and manufacturers all face margin compression if crude stays above $100.
US30.watch: Caterpillar and Boeing report mid-week. Guidance on fuel cost assumptions will tell if industrials can absorb $100+ oil or if earnings estimates need cutting.

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin topped $80,900 (+1.4%), its highest since January, as geopolitical stress drove flows into non-sovereign stores of value alongside gold.
BTC.why: The narrative has flipped: BTC is trading as digital gold during this crisis, not as a risk asset. ETF inflows accelerated for a third straight week.
BTC.watch: Dominance at 58.8% signals capital concentrating in BTC over alts. A clean break above $82K targets the January high at $84,500.

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: ETH rose 1.6% to $2,383, riding BTC's coattails but underperforming on a ratio basis as validators continued exiting the queue post-Pectra issues.
ETH.why: Ethereum's DeFi TVL is recovering from last month's exploit wave, but institutional confidence remains fragile until the validator exit queue normalizes.
ETH.watch: ETH/BTC ratio at 0.0295 — near cycle lows. A bounce here historically precedes alt season; continued breakdown opens 0.025.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVDA flatlined at $198.48 (+0.02%) — remarkably stable given the broad selloff — as AI spending commitments from hyperscalers reinforced the bull case.
NVDA.why: NVDA is now defensive tech: every cloud earnings call this season has raised AI capex guidance, guaranteeing Blackwell demand through 2027.
NVDA.watch: Taiwan Semi monthly revenue data due this week. Strong April numbers confirm the AI build-out pace; any softness triggers sympathy selling in NVDA.

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: Tesla edged up 0.4% to $392.51 despite EU regulatory headwinds — reports say Brussels is skeptical of Tesla's automated driving tech certification.
TSLA.why: FSD approval in Europe is the next major catalyst worth $50B+ in market cap. Regulatory skepticism delays the timeline but doesn't kill the thesis.
TSLA.watch: EU auto safety committee meets Wednesday. Any formal objection to Tesla's FSD filing caps upside at $400; clearance path opens the $420 breakout.

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple dropped 1.2% to $276.83 as iPhone supply chain exposure to Middle East shipping routes spooked investors after Friday's record close.
AAPL.why: Apple's Strait-dependent supply chain is the vulnerability. Components from India and Southeast Asia transit Hormuz. A disruption adds weeks to delivery timelines.
AAPL.watch: WWDC is a month away. Any early AI feature leaks could reignite the upgrade-cycle narrative and offset geopolitical drag.

Bank of America resets Apple stock price target after earnings

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: Microsoft slipped 0.2% to $413.62 — minimal damage as Azure AI revenue growth (60%+ YoY) continues to underpin the bull case post-earnings.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the safest mega-cap AI play: recurring enterprise revenue, growing Azure margins, and Copilot monetization just beginning.
MSFT.watch: Musk vs. Altman trial resumes today. Any OpenAI governance ruling could affect MSFT's $13B investment optionality.

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: META gained 0.3% to $610.41, quietly extending its run as AI ad-targeting improvements drove Q1 revenue above all estimates last week.
META.why: Meta's AI spend is actually paying off in core business — unlike peers spending on speculative AGI, Meta's models directly boost ad ROAS for advertisers.
META.watch: Instagram Reels monetization update at investor conference Thursday. Any CPM improvement data above 15% YoY extends META toward $650.

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.past: Alphabet fell 0.6% to $383.25 as antitrust trial headlines resurfaced — DOJ reportedly seeking behavioral remedies on default search deals.
GOOGL.why: Search default revenue ($20B+/yr from Apple alone) is at risk if the DOJ wins. Even a partial remedy reprices Alphabet's earnings power significantly.
GOOGL.watch: Judge's preliminary ruling expected by end of May. Any signal of structural vs. behavioral remedy determines whether $370 support holds.

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: Amazon surged 1.4% to $272.05 as the GameStop-eBay bid circus reminded investors that AMZN's e-commerce moat is unassailable — and AWS AI growth reaccelerated.
AMZN.why: AWS is the second-largest AI cloud. Bedrock model marketplace growth is accelerating, and retail margins expanding simultaneously create a double engine.
AMZN.watch: Prime Day dates expected this week. Any earlier-than-usual timing signals confidence in consumer spending despite energy price headwinds.
